content.explanation:
🖼️ Bringing Webpages to Life

Text and links are powerful, but websites become truly engaging when you add images.

Images are added using the <img> tag, which is different from most HTML tags because it does NOT have a closing tag.

🌐 Why Images Matter

Images help you:

  • 📌 Show products or ideas visually
  • 🎨 Improve design and engagement
  • 🧠 Communicate faster than text

A webpage without images often feels incomplete.
content.syntax_breakdown:
🧪 Image Tag Structure

The basic format is:

<img src="image-url" alt="description">

📌 Important Attributes

  • src: the image source (URL or file path)
  • alt: text shown if image fails + used for accessibility

🧠 Example

<img src="https://example.com/photo.jpg" alt="A sample image">

👉 Always include alt for better accessibility and SEO.

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
My Visual Web Page

This page will now include images.

mini_challenge.instruction: 🎯 Mission: Add your first image 1. Below the paragraph, add an tag 2. Use this image URL: https://via.placeholder.com/300 3. Add alt text: My First Image 👉 Then run your code and see the image appear
mini_challenge.hint: 👉 Example format: Remember: img tags do NOT need a closing tag.

quiz_engine.question: Why does the tag not require a closing tag?
options: Because images are automatically closed by browsers
Because it is a self-closing (empty) element that does not wrap content
Because HTML does not support closing tags
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 The tag is a self-closing element because it does not contain inner content like text or other elements.

takeaways: The tag is used to display images in HTML
It does not require a closing tag
Images make websites more engaging and visual

cheat_sheet.img_tag: Displays an image on a webpage
cheat_sheet.src: Image source URL or file path
